(i) b1_qdrant_unavailable_returns_bm25_hits_with_degraded_flag: bogus Qdrant URL (127.0.0.1:1) via new QdrantStore::open_lazy + OkEmbedder + SQLite FTS5 corpus -> envelope.degraded.qdrant_unavailable=true, hits non-empty, includes the "ollama" BM25 doc. (ii) b2_ollama_unavailable_returns_bm25_hits_with_degraded_flag: FlakyEmbedder always-Err + SQLite -> envelope.degraded.ollama_unavailable=true, hits include the "rust" BM25 doc; Qdrant never reached so qdrant_unavailable stays false. (iii) both_vector_lanes_dead_and_no_sqlite_returns_err_actionable: FlakyEmbedder + lazy Qdrant + no SQLite -> Err mentions "all retrieval lanes unavailable" + names which deps. (iv) bm25_only_envelope_has_qdrant_flag_set_but_returns_hits: per-call envelope semantics locked. Refactor: introduced Embedder trait (Rust 2024 native async-fn-in-traits) so HybridRecall<E: Embedder = OllamaClient> is generic for test injection; QdrantStore::open_lazy() builds gRPC client without ensure_collection; HybridRecall::search() returns RecallEnvelope { hits, degraded } and emits tracing::warn! lines (target="obelion.recall") on each lane failure + tracing::error! when both lanes die. MCP server now wires SQLite at startup so production has BM25 lane available for graceful degradation (resolve_sqlite_path: OBELION_DATA_DIR env or <config_dir>/obelion/obelion.db default). MCP recall tool description updated to advertise the envelope shape. verdict: 3/3 tests pass. (i) concurrent_multi_writer_no_panics_correct_total: 4 tokio spawn_blocking writers each running 250 insert_observation calls against the SAME SQLite file via independent SqliteStore handles (modeling separate processes); final count_observations()=1000 exact; verify_full_chain() passes — the hash chain is unbroken across all 1000 concurrent inserts. (ii) read_during_write_no_lock_panic: writer + reader tokio tasks hammer the same file simultaneously (writer doing inserts, reader doing bm25_search); WAL mode allows the concurrent reader without SQLITE_BUSY panic. (iii) wal_mode_is_actually_set_on_file_backed_store: PRAGMA journal_mode reports "wal" — locks the production posture contract. Discovered + fixed a real concurrency bug during the test: insert_observation was reading last_observation_hash() OUTSIDE the transaction, so two writers saw the same head and chain-forked (test caught it: "chain broken at index 56"). Fix: BEGIN IMMEDIATE wraps both the head-read and the row INSERT in one atomic transaction. SqliteStore::init_schema now sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL + synchronous=NORMAL + busy_timeout=5s on every open. Documented in docs/concurrency.md.

NEW tests/qa/c3_resource_footprint.ps1 harness re-runs the measurement + appends one row per metric to docs/perf-baseline.tsv on each release tag, providing future regression detection.
notes: tests/qa/c3_resource_footprint.ps1 + docs/perf-baseline.tsv

feature: cargo test -p obelion-core --test recall_degraded -- b3 && cargo test -p obelion-core --lib timeout
verdict: 5/5 timeout unit tests + 5/5 recall_degraded tests (4 existing + new b3_slow_embedder_times_out_and_flips_ollama_unavailable) all GREEN. The B3 test: OBELION_TIMEOUT_MS=150 + SlowEmbedder { delay_ms=2000 } + SQLite BM25 lane wired. recall().search() must (a) return within <1500 ms (well under the 2000 ms sleep), (b) set envelope.degraded.ollama_unavailable=true, (c) still populate hits via BM25. All asserts pass. New crate-level module crates/core/src/timeout.rs (74 lines): pub const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=4000, pub fn resolve_timeout() reads OBELION_TIMEOUT_MS with 100ms floor, pub fn parse_timeout(Option<&str>) is the pure-function impl (no env mutation in tests — avoids the test-parallel-env-race that the first cut hit), pub async fn with_timeout<F,T>(d, fut) wraps any Result-future with a hard cap; on Err returns "timeout after Xs" so the existing degraded-path match handles it like any other dep failure. recall.rs search() now wraps embed_query + qdrant.search in with_timeout(resolve_timeout(), ...) per call. Pattern composes cleanly with the B1+B2 envelope flags from commit 2a9c64d — timeouts are just another flavor of lane outage.
notes: crates/core/src/timeout.rs + crates/core/src/recall.rs + crates/core/src/lib.rs + crates/core/tests/recall_degraded.rs
